rationale: Authority data for W40K-0091..W40K-0100 — the 2017-2019 cluster bracketing the 8th-edition / Dark-Imperium pivot. Bookends: Andy Clark's *Shroud of Night* (2017, Alpha Legion infiltration on the oceanic hive world Tsadrekha during the Noctis Aeterna, Khârn the Betrayer-led rival assault, Unsung warband + Strike-Captain Kassar) and Andy Clark's *Celestine: The Living Saint* (2019, dual-strand novel about Saint Celestine in Imperium Nihilus + her allegorical torment-realm). Heart of the batch: Robbie MacNiven's *The Last Hunt* (2017, White-Scars 4th Company under Joghaten Khan on Honour-World Darkand vs. Tyranids + Genestealer Cults + uneasy Aeldari/Drukhari alliance), Rob Sanders' *Sons of the Hydra* (2017, Alpha-Legion warband 'the Redacted' under Occam the Untrue, twisty Maelstrom infiltration), Josh Reynolds' *Lukas the Trickster* (2018, Space-Wolves trickster-duel between Lukas the Strifeson and Drukhari Duke Sliscus on Fenris during Helwinter), Gav Thorpe's *Imperator: Wrath of the Omnissiah* (2018, Legio Metalica's Imperator-titan *Casus Belli* + Magos Exasus on Nicomedua — 'Hunt for Red October with an Imperator Titan'), Mike Lee's *Legacy of Dorn* (2018, Crimson-Fists Veteran-Sergeant Sandor Galleas guerrilla campaign during the ork invasion of Rynn's World), Robbie MacNiven's *Blood of Iax* (2018, Primaris-only Ultramarines Strike Force Fulminata — brothers Chaplain Kastor + Apothecary Polixis — vs Warlord Urgork on Ikara IX in the Dark-Imperium era), Chris Wraight's *The Lords of Silence* (2018, Death-Guard warband under First-Captain Vorx + Slert + Philemon, warp-stranded grand-cruiser *Solace*, 13th-Black-Crusade-aimed-at-Ultramar setup, whodunnit register), and Aaron Dembski-Bowden's *Spear of the Emperor* (2018, helot-narrator Anuradha relaying Mentor-Legion Lieutenant Amadeus Kaias Incarius's crossing of the Great Rift into the Elara's Veil nebula to assess the Emperor's Spears + Star Scorpions + Celestial Lions chapters). Structural notes: dense first-authority-layer surface-form additions (Alpha Legion as primary protagonist faction, Death Guard as primary protagonist faction, Crimson Fists chapter as primary protagonist faction, White Scars chapter as primary protagonist faction, Death-Guard-/Nurgle-/Word-Bearers Chaos surface forms, Primaris-Astartes register, Tsadrekha / Darkand / Nicomedua / Rynn's World / Ikara IX / Elara's Veil + Solace / Casus Belli named-vessel surface forms, Khârn the Betrayer + Duke Sliscus + Urgork + Vorx + Lukas the Strifeson + Galleas + Kastor + Polixis + Amadeus + Anuradha + Celestine + Magos Exasus + Joghaten Khan + Occam the Untrue + Kassar + Slert + Philemon character surface forms). One `data_conflict { field: format, suggestion: short_novel/novella }` flag on Celestine (Lexicanum + Track-of-Words consistently characterise it as 'short novel' / novella-length; the SSOT format=`novel` is plausibly correct but worth flagging). No `low_confidence` flags this iteration — all ten entries have strong post-2017 Black-Library coverage. **Resolver-Pre-Warning note:** cumulativeBefore=90 (Brief-061 algorithm's strict reading: 90 % 50 ≠ 0 → no pause); after this batch, cumulativeAfter=100, which DOES trigger the loud-stop in the NEXT iteration (`ssot-w40k-011`). User explicitly requested this batch, so produced normally; the resolver-pause-block will fire on the subsequent iteration.

overrides.synopsis: During the Noctis Aeterna, the oceanic hive world Tsadrekha holds an astropathic beacon bright enough to keep the warp-storm dark at bay. Strike-Captain Kassar and the Unsung, an Alpha Legion warband stranded since the Scouring, are sent to seize and corrupt it, but Kharn the Betrayer turns the mission into a many-sided siege. Imperial Fists, Sororitas, Guardsmen and a saint defend the prize while rival Chaos warbands crowd the battlefield. A twisty Alpha Legion infiltration novel where freedom depends on lying faster than every other killer in the dark.

On the steppe honour-world Darkand, the Great Rift has broken the skies and Hive Fleet Leviathan is already inside the gates: genestealer cultists have hollowed out the PDF and nobility before the Tyranids strike. Joghaten Khan leads the White Scars 4th Company to defend a world bound to the chapter by oath and blood. Then Aeldari and Drukhari forces offer help for reasons of their own, forcing the Khan to choose between old hatred, warrior honour, and a xenos bargain that may be the only way Darkand lives. A fast White Scars war story about trust at the edge of extinction.

Occam the Untrue commands the Redacted, a small Alpha Legion warband stitched together from legionaries, renegade Astartes and human Hydra cultists in the Maelstrom. Their next infiltration should be another exercise in lies and misdirection, but Inquisition hunters are closing in, the warband's cover is fraying, and even Occam can no longer tell whether his orders come from the Legion, a rival cell or a deeper game. The mission drives them toward the cold heart of the galaxy, where every false face carries another false face beneath it. A deception-as-warfare novel for readers who like their Chaos plots knotted.

Helwinter has frozen Fenris and Lukas the Strifeson has once again worn out a Wolf Lord's patience. Reassigned to Kjarl Grimblood's Great Company, the Space Wolves' Jackalwolf is barely through the ritual humiliation before Duke Sliscus and his Drukhari raiders descend to hunt legendary prey. Lukas leads Blood Claws and Fenrisian tribes through ambushes, baited traps and folk-tale reversals while Sliscus answers prank with atrocity. Half trickster duel, half chapter argument about whether genius excuses disorder in the ranks.

The Casus Belli, a ten-thousand-year-old Imperator Titan of Legio Metalica, marches onto the forge world Nicomedua as a tech-priest secession mutates into open war. Warlord and Reaver Titans, Knight households and skitarii legions clash with corrupted engines, traitor Astartes and cult armies under the shadow of the Cicatrix Maledictum. Inside the god-machine, Magos Exasus has a quieter battlefield: someone in the crew hierarchy is sabotaging the Titan from within. A Titan-war procedural with the scale of a walking city and the tension of a naval mutiny mystery.

When the ork invasion of Rynn's World destroys Arx Tyrannus and leaves the Crimson Fists almost broken, Veteran-Sergeant Sandor Galleas drops behind enemy lines with only a battered Sternguard squad and human survivors from ruined hive-cities. Galleas believes Crimson Fists doctrine is Imperial Fists doctrine perfected, but the wasteland teaches him a different war: ambush, retreat, hunger, civilian courage and losses counted week by week. A successor-chapter survival story about wearing a siege tradition while learning to fight like a guerrilla.

In the Dark Imperium era, Strike Force Fulminata brings a Primaris-only Ultramarines force to the ork-held mining world Ikara IX. At its heart are blood-brothers who serve the chapter in opposite ways: Chaplain Kastor, all fire and charge, and Apothecary Polixis, calm guardian of gene-seed and wounded warriors. When Warlord Urgork proves far more cunning than expected and aims his plan at the Apothecary rather than the usual glory targets, the brothers' bond becomes the campaign's hinge. Multi-POV battle fiction with Reivers, Astra Militarum defenders and ork viewpoints orbiting a very Ultramarines question of duty.

First-Captain Vorx leads the Lords of Silence, a Death Guard warband sailing with Abaddon's Thirteenth Black Crusade, when their grand cruiser Solace is flung off course into a nameless stretch of the warp. Vorx, Biologus Putrifier Slert and Tallyman Philemon must discover where they are, how to rejoin the war for Ultramar, and which of their own made the ship jump. A Word Bearers encounter turns the problem into an uneasy Chaos alliance, but the real pleasure is stranger: plague monks conducting a slow whodunnit inside Astartes ranks. Patient, rancid and oddly funny, this is Death Guard as implacable routine horror.

Anuradha, helot to Lieutenant Amadeus Kaias Incarius of the Mentors, tells this crossing of the Great Rift from decades of hindsight. Amadeus is sent into Elara's Veil to judge three frontier chapters: the failing Star Scorpions, the Inquisition-wounded Celestial Lions, and the Emperor's Spears, who still guard a region the wider Imperium can no longer reach. He arrives as a polished Primaris emissary and finds his chapter's reputation wins him suspicion rather than welcome. As the way home vanishes, observer and servant are pulled into the Spears' doomed vigil. A frontier-Astartes memoir where the armour is seen from below.

Every time Saint Celestine dies, she falls into a realm of torment and must fight her way back to the Emperor's wars. This short novel braids that inner trial with a desperate battle in Imperium Nihilus, where Celestine manifests beside Sisters of Battle, Astra Militarum soldiers and fanatical civilians beneath the Noctis Aeterna. The outer story is last-stand war; the inner one asks what centuries of resurrection do to faith when the reward for dying is only more dying. A saint's legend retold as philosophical horror.

flags.reason: Lexicanum + Track-of-Words + Black Library product page consistently characterise *Celestine* as a 'short novel' / novella-length release (closer to the *Assassinorum: Execution Force* register from ssot-w40k-009 than a standard novel). Valid `book_format` enum has `novella` but no `short_novel`.
